body_txt: Everybody knows Chainsaw Good . When designing a character, spinning blades are a hallmark of a cool or otherwise scary design, making them especially popular in action and horror settings. Chainsaws are the pinnacle of Awesome, yet Impractical , yet some creators like to take this a step farther; making the weapon part of the character itself . Sawblade Anatomy is exactly what its name suggests; a spinning sawblade , chainsaw, or similar device that forms part of a character's anatomy, used as though it was another organ or appendage. Most often, this trope can be found as part of a robot or a cybernetic implant ; however cases in which the blade/apparatus is natural is not off the table. Expect this trope to be paired with villains or otherwise imposing characters. See Detachable Blades for use cases in which the sawblade can be removed or detached from the character at will. Compare Blade Below the Shoulder in which the weapon is a non-spinning blade. Contrast Buzzsaw Jaw , which is merely a Metaphoric Metamorphosis applied to a Big Eater .
